title: Sweet Peruvian Fruit Soup
description: A light, festive, low‑fat dessert featuring tropical fruits, a splash of lime, and a velvety swirl of crème fraîche, all simmered quickly in a pressure cooker.
instructions: Rinse and dice all fruits, keeping the mango, pineapple, papaya, and banana in a medium bowl.
In the pressure cooker insert the fruits, lime zest, lime juice, light coconut milk, maple syrup, cinnamon stick, and the split vanilla bean.
Seal the lid, set to high pressure, and cook for 3 minutes.
Quick‑release the pressure, open the lid, and stir in the low‑fat crème fraîche until the soup is smooth.
Taste; if extra sweetness is desired, drizzle a little more maple syrup.
Transfer to serving bowls, swirl a small spoonful of crème fraîche on top, and optionally garnish with a sprinkle of toasted coconut flakes.
Serve immediately warm, or chill in the fridge for 30 minutes before serving if a cooler dessert is preferred.
